title: PTD Today — Media

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Gen Z is increasingly struggling with loneliness and dating app Hinge is expanding its social impact efforts. Hinge announced a $1 million fund to support social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London to host free or affordable events for young people under its One More Hour initiative.
bullets: Gen Z experiencing rising loneliness
Hinge expanding its One More Hour social impact program
$1 million fund announced for social groups
Target cities: New York, Los Angeles, London
Funds intended for free or affordable youth social events
takeaways: The initiative targets New York, Los Angeles and London—local infrastructure planners in these metros should note platform-led community programs as part of the ecosystem.
Digital platforms funding in-person events can be a factor in localized spikes in app usage or service demand; include such initiatives in capacity and demand reviews.
Partnerships between apps and community groups may influence timing and localization of digital services, relevant for operational scheduling and resource allocation.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This session shows how to automatically build a knowledge graph from raw documents and connect it to an AI agent so the agent understands your business. The presenters walk an end-to-end demo that requires no graph expertise.
bullets: Knowledge graphs are presented as a solution for AI agents that lack company-specific knowledge.
The talk demonstrates automatic construction of a knowledge graph from raw documents.
The knowledge graph is connected to an AI agent so it can use business-specific information.
No graph queries or graph theory expertise required for the workflow shown.
An end-to-end build is demonstrated live in the session.
takeaways: Enables domain-aware AI agents that can answer questions about your organization using existing documents.
Automating graph construction reduces the need to train or hire graph specialists.
An end-to-end approach can accelerate deployment of business-specific AI capabilities for operations and incident response.
Leverages existing document stores to make AI agents more useful without heavy tooling or theory investment.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Randa Barsoum, Tax Partner at PwC US, highlights tax credits and incentives for sustainability beyond the usual energy-focused measures — including state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation like reimagining packaging or processes. The core message: viewing sustainability investments through a broader tax lens can unlock meaningful tax value for businesses.
bullets: Energy incentives are common but only part of the available tax landscape.
State and local abatements can provide project-level tax relief.
Location-based incentives may influence siting and investment decisions.
R&D tax credits can apply to sustainability-driven innovation in processes or design.
A broader tax-incentive view can increase the value of sustainability investments.
takeaways: Include state and local abatements and location-based incentives in site-selection and expansion analyses for grid/data center projects.
Document process and design innovations tied to sustainability to help substantiate potential R&D tax credit claims.
Build a comprehensive tax-incentive review into CapEx and sustainability business cases to improve project economics.
Engage tax advisors early to identify incentive opportunities beyond energy credits and integrate them into decision-making.

items.title: Copilot helps Christa find her inner star 🌟

ai.summary: Based on the available description, the video shows Copilot helping Christa discover her inner star against imagery of a sky full of stars. It invites viewers to download and try Copilot via the link in bio.
bullets: Visual motif: a sky full of stars.
Copilot helps Christa 'find the star within.'
Uses an uplifting, empowering tone (star emoji present).
Call to action: download and try Copilot via link in bio.
takeaways: Human-centered messaging (e.g., 'find your inner star') can help drive interest and adoption of tools.
Clear, simple CTAs like 'download & try' are effective for encouraging trials.
Relatable visual storytelling can support internal and external communications when introducing new technology.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description… NVIDIA’s GeForce Garage highlights the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build, u/JeffersonPutnam, who built a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 system. The video description lists the full parts: Fractal Design Era 2 chassis, GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition, MSI MPG B850I Edge, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO, plus a PCPartPicker link and social links.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam — SFF GeForce RTX 5090 community build
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2; GPU: RTX 5090 Founders Edition
Core components: MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard and Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU
Memory and power: 32GB Teamgroup DDR5-6000 and Corsair SF1000 SFX PSU
PCPartPicker list provided and NVIDIA social links/hashtags included
takeaways: Showcases a flagship RTX 5090 installed in a small-form-factor (Era 2) chassis — highlights component choices for high-performance compact builds.
The build pairs a top-tier GPU with a 1000W SFX PSU (Corsair SF1000) and a 240mm AIO (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS), underscoring power and cooling requirements for similar systems.
Uses a mini-ITX motherboard (MSI MPG B850I Edge) with a high-performance Ryzen 7 9800X3D, demonstrating tight CPU/GPU integration in compact form factors.
PCPartPicker parts list is provided for exact specifications and procurement reference.
This is a community-focused showcase (Summer of RTX contest) used to promote GeForce RTX 50-series and engagement channels.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description, TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy AI solutions that unlocked new revenue streams. They leveraged GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure to streamline operations, boost developer productivity, and compress their digital transformation timeline.
bullets: TTEC worked with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
The initiative unlocked entirely new revenue streams for TTEC.
GenAI plus Google Cloud infrastructure were used to streamline operations.
Developer productivity was improved, helping speed delivery of solutions.
Google Cloud Consulting helped compress the digital transformation timeline and expand business ambitions.
takeaways: Engaging cloud consulting partners can shorten AI deployment timelines and speed time-to-value.
Robust cloud infrastructure is a foundation for using GenAI to create new revenue opportunities.
Improvements in developer productivity and operational streamlining reduce time-to-market and operational friction.
Consulting support can enable bolder transformation goals beyond incremental modernization efforts.
